name: supergraph
tagline: Evidence-gated coding workflows powered by Codebase Memory MCP.
description:
Supergraph is an evidence-gated AI coding workflow powered by Codebase Memory MCP >= 0.9.0 and optional Serena LSP.

WORKFLOW

  scan → analyze → plan → TDD → execute → fix → verify → review

Codebase Memory indexes each repository locally under a stable project identity and supplies blast radius, callers/callees, architecture clusters, dependency cycles, test gaps, complexity hotspots, and changed-symbol evidence. Serena adds LSP references and diagnostics when installed.

Evidence gates prevent production edits without a RED test, execution without an approved plan, and completion claims without fresh verification and independent review. Hooks provide session reminders, plan guards, failure hints, and asynchronous graph freshness through Codebase Memory auto-watch.

WINDOWS HOOKS

run-hook.cmd dynamically resolves Git Bash from CLAUDE_CODE_GIT_BASH_PATH, system Git, user-level Git, or where git.exe. If Git Bash is unavailable it prints "supergraph: Git Bash not found — hooks skipped" and exits successfully.

SUPPORTED STACKS

Auto-detected: Node.js/TypeScript, Flutter/Dart, PHP, Python, Go, Rust. Specialized skills cover Flutter review, database migrations, frontend UI, and Playwright testing.

skills.name: scan
skills.description: Run this first, every session. Builds the codebase graph, detects your project language and test/lint tools, activates Serena if available, and saves environment state to .supergraph-env. Subsequent skills read from this context instead of re-scanning. Required by all other skills.

skills.name: analyze
skills.description: Use before planning when the task is ambiguous or touches hub/bridge nodes. Runs a structured grill (up to 3 focused questions with recommended answers), computes blast radius, identifies risky nodes, and selects the safest approach. Reads and updates CONTEXT.md with new domain terms.

skills.name: plan
skills.description: Turns a requirement into a machine-readable plan with TDD steps, blast radius per task, and risk flags. Saved to docs/supergraph/plans/ so work can be resumed across sessions or picked up by a teammate. Requires user approval before execution starts.

skills.name: tdd
skills.description: Enforces strict test-driven development per task: write a failing test (RED), write the minimum code to pass it (GREEN), clean up without breaking it (REFACTOR), then commit. Production code without a verified failing test first is blocked.

skills.name: execute
skills.description: Takes a saved plan file and runs all tasks — independent tasks in parallel, dependent tasks in sequence. Each task goes through the full TDD cycle and gets its own commit. Stops and reports if any task gets stuck after 3 retries.

skills.name: fix
skills.description: Runs after all coding is done. Executes tests, lint, and format in a loop — if anything fails, fixes the issue and retries. Also checks the graph for new circular dependencies or surprise connections introduced by the change. Stops after 3 failed iterations and asks the user.

skills.name: integration
skills.description: Runs integration and end-to-end tests after unit tests pass. Auto-detects test runners (Jest, Vitest, pytest, Cypress, Playwright) and docker-compose environments. Skipped automatically when no integration test config is found.

skills.name: verify
skills.description: Blocks any completion claim unless backed by fresh evidence: test output, lint pass, build log. Claude cannot say 'done', 'fixed', or 'ready' without running this first. Prevents false-positive sign-offs that pass review but fail in CI.

skills.name: review
skills.description: Spawns an independent reviewer agent that cannot see the session history — only the diff and the graph. Checks for correctness, graph regressions (new hub node edits, circular deps, surprise connections), and security patterns. Returns a clear verdict: APPROVED, NEEDS_CHANGES, or BLOCKED.

skills.name: diagnose
skills.description: Use when a bug exists but the cause is unknown. Runs a 6-phase loop: build a feedback loop to reproduce reliably → generate 3–5 ranked falsifiable hypotheses → instrument one variable at a time → apply fix → write regression test → post-mortem. Avoids the shotgun approach of changing multiple things at once.

skills.name: zoom-out
skills.description: Generates a one-shot domain-vocabulary module map of the codebase. Uses graph community detection to identify clusters, then labels each with plain-English domain terms from CONTEXT.md. Useful when disoriented after a deep-dive or when onboarding to an unfamiliar repo.

skills.name: architecture
skills.description: Produces a self-contained HTML report with Mermaid before/after diagrams, recommendation strength badges, and a structured grill on architectural decisions. Designed for pre-refactor planning, team onboarding, and architecture review meetings.

skills.name: prd
skills.description: Converts an informal conversation into a structured Product Requirements Document: problem statement, proposed solution, user stories, acceptance criteria, and explicit out-of-scope items. Optionally posts to GitHub Issues with a 'ready-for-agent' label so it feeds directly into the triage pipeline.

skills.name: triage
skills.description: Processes a list of issues through a formal state machine: needs-triage → needs-info → ready-for-agent → ready-for-human → wontfix. Each classification includes reasoning. Issues marked 'ready-for-agent' are formatted as self-contained inputs for the supergraph pipeline.

skills.name: prototype
skills.description: Generates throwaway code to validate an approach before committing to a full plan. Two branches: Logic (terminal state machine) for backend approaches, UI (multiple designs on one route) for frontend options. No tests, no persistence — explicitly deleted after a winner is chosen.

skills.name: handoff
skills.description: Compresses the full session state into a compact file: active plan tasks with status, uncommitted changes, key decisions made, and exact next steps. Designed for when the context window is running out or when switching to a new session. The next session reads the handoff file as its first step.

skills.name: caveman
skills.description: Activates ~75% output compression across all skills. Strips explanatory prose, redundant formatting, and filler phrases — keeps code, numbers, file paths, and error messages exact. Auto-suspends for safety warnings. Trigger with the word 'caveman' or set SUPERGRAPH_CAVEMAN=true in .supergraph-env to persist across sessions.

skills.name: serena
skills.description: Foundation skill for Serena MCP integration. Provides setup instructions, tool reference, and the memory workflow for storing LSP findings. Other skills call Serena tools automatically when available: find_referencing_symbols before any rename, get_diagnostics_for_file during fix, replace_symbol_body for targeted edits.

skills.name: flutter-dart-code-review
skills.description: Library-agnostic review checklist for Flutter/Dart codebases. Covers 15 sections: widget lifecycle, build method hygiene, state management patterns (BLoC, Riverpod, Provider, GetX, MobX, Signals), Dart idioms, performance (unnecessary rebuilds, const usage), accessibility, security, and static analysis integration.

skills.name: database-migrations
skills.description: Guides safe, reversible schema changes with graph-informed blast radius checks before every migration. Covers expand-contract for zero-downtime deploys, backfill strategies for large tables, and rollback scripts at each step. Supports PostgreSQL, Prisma, Drizzle, Kysely, Django ORM, TypeORM, and golang-migrate.

skills.name: frontend-design
skills.description: Generates production-grade frontend code with intentional, distinctive aesthetics — not the generic AI look. Focuses on typographic hierarchy, deliberate color systems, spatial composition, and motion that communicates meaning. For React, Next.js, and plain HTML/CSS.

skills.name: webapp-testing
skills.description: Tests running web applications using Playwright (via MCP or script). Recon-first: waits for networkidle before interacting, discovers real selectors from rendered state rather than guessing from source, then verifies all flows affected by the current change.

changelog.2.2.0: Added 8 new skills: diagnose (6-phase structured debugging), handoff (session compaction), triage (issue state machine), caveman (~75% token compression), prd (conversation → PRD → GitHub Issue), architecture (HTML + Mermaid report), prototype (throwaway validation), zoom-out (domain module map). Added CONTEXT.md shared vocabulary system — analyze, plan, and review read/write it to reduce cross-session inconsistency. Added 4 smart automation hooks: SessionStart (loads vocab, reminds about handoff, activates caveman flag), PostToolUse Bash (detects test failures → suggests diagnose), PreCompact (urgent handoff reminder with plan status), UserPromptSubmit (detects caveman/triage trigger phrases).
changelog.2.1.0: Integrated Serena MCP across all skills — diagnostics, symbol navigation, safe rename/replace. Added /supergraph:serena foundation skill. Optimized skills for token efficiency: flutter-dart-code-review and database-migrations reduced by ~85% with reference content in separate companion files. Scan now reuses .supergraph-env when branch unchanged.
changelog.2.0.2: Added Flutter/Dart code review checklist (15 sections), frontend design guidelines, and webapp testing toolkit with Playwright.
changelog.2.0.0: Initial public release. 9 core workflow skills, 4 specialized agents, graph MCP integration, auto language detection for Node.js, Flutter, and PHP.
